Fanlistings, Site Depolyment, and Some Personal Updates

Posted May 20, 2026
Tagged:
  • #autism

First off, I've started my own fanlisting collective and kicked it off by starting a fanlisting for Balatro! I'll hopefully add dklsfjasldfk eventually.

I want to give a huge shout out to Leilukin! A while back they gave me some tech help over email, and recently they made the source code for their fanlisting collective public, which was an invaluable reference when it came to making my own since I suck at using PHP.

Read More

ABQ Island Photodump

Posted Apr 13, 2026
Tagged:
  • #gaming
  • #Tomodachi Life
  • #Breaking Bad
  • #Better Call Saul
  • #photodump

⚠️ This post may contain minor spoilers for Breaking Bad and Better Call Saul. (Yes, really.)

I've been so excited for the full release of Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream! Unfortunately, while I was playing the demo I bought an item from the clothing shop, which effectively ends the demo and keeps all mii events from happening. I can't do anything until the full game actually releases and I can transfer my save data.

So, to quench my extreme thirst for Tomodachi Life content, I booted up what I thought was a fresh save for the old Tomodachi Life on 3DS, only to realize that I already started a save file for the game about a year ago.

Read More

10/10 Songs

Posted Apr 8, 2026
Tagged:
  • #personal
  • #music
  • #notes

A list of my all time favorite songs. Songs where I think if anything about it was changed whatsoever it would make that song worse.

  • Magdalena Bay - Death & Romance (especially with the transition to "Fear, Sex", as heard in the music video)
  • Magdalena Bay - You Lose!
  • MGMT - Time to Pretend
  • MGMT - Kids

I know there's more but I'm blanking out at the moment. Might edit this post more in the future.

March has been a little rough for me. I didn't do that much, honestly. I got sick multiple times and my flare ups have also been pretty bad. I've been exhausted and not as motivated to work on anything, but I'm hoping to turn that around in April.

Tagged:

I'm always looking for new blogs to follow via RSS and a good way to do that is to search through the various blog directories out there. Most directories are sorted by topic and I've come to realize that I'm a lot more picky than I thought!

I'll be honest, February wasn't nearly as productive as I would have liked it to be. My mind was mostly focused on just "making it through the day/week/month". I had a whole list of things I was supposed to get done in my bullet journal (which I was too burnt out to use for most of the month) and I think I only crossed out two or three.

I started collecting itemLabel plushies last year. I wanted to include some recent photos of them in my next monthly recap post, but I realized that I had so many stashed away in my phone that I should probably share the old ones first. So This post will just be a dump of those, along with my assorted thoughts.

Read More

"Recession Indicators"

Posted Feb 25, 2026
Tagged:
  • #random
  • #politics

Everything's supposedly a "recession indicator" nowadays. "recession indicator" this, "recession indicator" that, it's usually over pretty frivolous stuff too. I always take the phrase with a grain of salt.

But the other day I got one of those "cash for gold" type ads before a YouTube video. I haven't even one of those since Obama was in office! A true, actual, genuine recession indicator if I've ever seen one. We are so cooked!

Conservatory Photodump

Posted Feb 19, 2026
Tagged:
  • #photodump

  • #going places

I got to go on a surprise trip to the conservatory the other day! We met up with a family friend there. I took a bunch of photos while I was there too, so I thought I might as well share them here!

Wizard-Style vs Witch-Style

Posted Feb 14, 2026
Tagged:
  • #stream of consciousness
  • #videos
  • #worldbuilding
  • #crochet

In my January recap, I linked to a video I enjoyed called "Wizard Cooking vs Witch Cooking". The video talks about different methods of cooking in real life and how to apply them in a fictional/world building context. It uses wizards and witches as a metaphor for strictly following the (cook)book vs just eyeballing ingredients as you go along.

While the video mostly sticks to talking about cooking there were some things that made me wonder how this kind of thinking could be applied to other activities as well.

Read More
